name: IBM Watson Language Translator V3 Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in the IBM Watson Language Translator V3 API

terms.definition: A globally unique string that identifies a translation model, such as 'en-es' for English to Spanish. Custom model IDs are generated by the service.
The model ID of the model used as the foundation for customization. Custom models are built on top of IBM-provided base models.
An approach to machine translation that uses artificial neural networks to predict the likelihood of a sequence of words, translating entire sentences at once rather than word-by-word.

A file containing source and target language term pairs that override domain translation data. Ensuring specific terms are always translated the same way regardless of context. Supports TMX or CSV format.
A file containing pairs of source and target language sentences used to train custom translation models. The file format must be TMX.
Translation Memory eXchange format. An XML standard for exchanging translation data between applications. Used for forced glossaries and parallel corpora in Language Translator.
A code identifying a human language, following ISO 639-1 conventions (e.g., 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish, 'zh' for Chinese).
The language of the input text to be translated. Can be specified explicitly or detected automatically by the service.
The language into which the source text will be translated.
The automatic identification of the language used in a given input text, with confidence scores for each candidate language.
A numeric value between 0 and 1 representing the service's certainty in its identification or translation. Higher values indicate greater confidence.
The capability to translate entire documents (PDF, Word, PowerPoint, etc.) while preserving the document's structure and formatting.
A required query parameter on all API calls specifying the API release date to use (e.g., 2018-05-01). This enables forward compatibility.
IBM Cloud Identity and Access Management authentication. API calls are authenticated with Bearer tokens obtained from the IAM service using an API key.
A property of translation models indicating whether the model can be used as a base for creating custom translation models.
The model automatically selected when only source and target language codes are specified without a specific model_id.
The subject area or industry context for which a translation model is optimized. The general domain is the most common.
A system-generated unique identifier for a document submitted for translation. Used to check status and retrieve the translated document.
A language property indicating whether words in the language are separated by whitespace, as in English and Spanish, versus character-based languages like Chinese.
The reading direction of a language: 'left_to_right' (e.g., English, Spanish) or 'right_to_left' (e.g., Arabic, Hebrew).
